prompt: Extract the name, age, and city from this sentence and return them as JSON with keys "name", "age", "city": "Maria Lopez is 34 and lives in Seville."

Convert this list into a JSON array of objects with keys "item" and "qty":
- apples x4
- bread x2
- milk x1
Return a JSON object mapping each US state abbreviation to its full name for: CA, TX, NY.

Format the following as a Markdown table with columns Name, Role, Start Year:
Alice, Engineer, 2019; Bob, Designer, 2021; Carol, PM, 2018.
Parse this log line into JSON with keys "timestamp", "level", "message": "2026-06-11T09:14:02Z ERROR disk full on /dev/sda1".
Produce a YAML document describing a person with fields name (string), languages (list), and active (boolean), for: Yuki, speaks Japanese and English, currently active.
Given the CSV header "id,price,qty" and the row "7,3.50,4", return a JSON object with the row's values and an added "total" field equal to price*qty.
Return strictly valid JSON: an object with key "primes" whose value is an array of the first five prime numbers.
Extract all email addresses from this text and return them as a JSON array of strings: "Contact ana@example.com or sales@store.co for help; ignore noreply@."
Convert this key:value text into a JSON object: "name: Orion; type: telescope; aperture_mm: 130; price: 249.99".
Return a JSON array of objects with keys "day" and "high_c" for: Mon 21, Tue 23, Wed 19.
Reformat this address into JSON with keys "street", "city", "state", "zip": "742 Evergreen Terrace, Springfield, IL 62704".
Given "3 red apples, 2 green pears, 5 yellow bananas", return a JSON array of objects with keys "count", "color", "fruit".
Produce a Markdown table from this data with columns Country, Capital: France/Paris, Japan/Tokyo, Kenya/Nairobi.
Return strictly valid JSON describing a book with keys "title", "author", "year", "genres" (array): Dune by Frank Herbert, 1965, science fiction and adventure.
Parse "GET /api/users 200 134ms" into JSON with keys "method", "path", "status" (int), "latency_ms" (int).

Convert this Markdown bullet list into a JSON array of strings:
- onboard new hire
- update docs
- review PR #42
Return a JSON object with keys "min", "max", "mean" computed from these numbers: 4, 8, 15, 16, 23, 42.
Given "John Smith, 555-0142, john@acme.com", return JSON with keys "name", "phone", "email".
Produce a TOML snippet for a config with a [server] table containing host = "0.0.0.0" and port = 8080.
Extract the hashtags from this post and return them as a JSON array (without the # symbol): "Loved the trip! #travel #Japan #foodie".
Return strictly valid JSON: an array of three objects, each with keys "n" and "square", for n = 1, 2, 3.
Convert this 24-hour time list into a JSON array of 12-hour strings with AM/PM: 09:00, 13:30, 23:45.
Given the sentence "The order #1007 shipped on 2026-05-02 to Berlin.", return JSON with keys "order_id" (int), "ship_date", "destination".
Format these as a JSON object mapping ISO currency code to symbol: USD, EUR, GBP, JPY.
Return a Markdown table with columns Task, Status, Owner from: deploy/done/Sam, tests/in progress/Lee, docs/todo/Kim.
Parse "v2.4.1" into JSON with keys "major", "minor", "patch" (all integers).
Convert this comma list into a JSON object counting each item: "apple, apple, pear, apple, pear".
Return strictly valid JSON describing an event with keys "name", "date", "attendees" (array of strings): Launch Party, 2026-07-01, attendees Ada and Grace and Alan.
Given "lat 37.7749, lon -122.4194", return JSON with numeric keys "latitude" and "longitude".
Convert this recipe line into JSON with keys "ingredient", "amount", "unit": "2 cups flour".
Return a JSON array of objects with keys "word" and "length" for each word in: "the quick brown fox".
Produce strictly valid JSON for a traffic light state machine: an object mapping each color ("red", "green", "yellow") to the next color.
Extract numbers from "Room 204, floor 2, 35 seats" and return JSON with keys "room", "floor", "seats" (all ints).
Convert this name list into a JSON array of objects with keys "first" and "last": "Ada Lovelace, Alan Turing, Grace Hopper".
Return a Markdown table of the first four powers of two with columns Exponent and Value (2^0 through 2^3).
Given "temp=21C humidity=48% wind=12kph", return JSON with keys "temp_c", "humidity_pct", "wind_kph" (all ints).
Return strictly valid JSON: an object with key "fibonacci" whose value is an array of the first eight Fibonacci numbers.
Convert this schedule into a JSON array of objects with keys "start", "end", "title": "09:00-10:00 Standup; 10:00-11:30 Design review".
Parse the query string "?page=2&sort=name&desc=true" into a JSON object of its parameters.
Given "Invoice 88: 3 widgets at $12.50 each", return JSON with keys "invoice" (int), "quantity" (int), "unit_price" (number), "total" (number).
Return a JSON object grouping these animals by class: "shark, sparrow, salmon, eagle" into keys "fish" and "bird".
Convert "RGB(255, 99, 71)" into a JSON object with keys "r", "g", "b" and an added "hex" field.
Return strictly valid JSON for a to-do item with keys "id", "text", "done" (boolean): id 5, "buy milk", not done.
Format this comparison as a Markdown table with columns Plan, Price, Seats: Free/$0/1, Team/$20/5, Business/$50/20.
Given "The package weighs 2.5 kg and costs 9.99 EUR", return JSON with keys "weight_kg" (number), "price" (number), "currency".
Convert this list of pairs into a JSON object: "a=1, b=2, c=3".
Return a JSON array of objects with keys "letter" and "position" for each letter in the word "cat" (1-indexed).
Parse "2026-06-11 14:05:09" into JSON with keys "year", "month", "day", "hour", "minute", "second" (all ints).
Return strictly valid JSON describing a playlist with keys "name" and "tracks" (array of objects with "title" and "minutes"): "Focus", with Rain (3) and Drift (5).
